data.title: ASTOUND Group Appoints Professional Sports Team Executive Heather Clayton as CFO
data.description: In a move that highlights ASTOUND Group's dedication to growth, particularly in mergers and acquisitions, new business ventures, and in-house intellectual property development in critical markets, the organization has brought on Heather Clayton as Chief Financial Officer. This strategic hire comes on the heels of a recent influx of leadership in the sports division. The hire of Clayton underscores ASTOUND's focus on expanding its reach and operations. The appointment was made public in Las Vegas on April 2, 2026.

data.title: Ether's price may hit new 2026 lows if bulls can't sustain $2.4K support. Break below $1,736 could be looming.
data.description: Ether price is at risk of dropping to new lows below $1,736 if bulls are unable to maintain the altcoin’s price above a critical ascending trendline.

data.title: FROM PLATFORM WARS TO INFRASTRUCTURE WARS: Hop-on's Digitalage Defines Stateful Media Infrastructure
data.description: HPNN has recently introduced Digitalage, a revolutionary platform that transforms live streams into profitable assets right from their inception. This groundbreaking technology is reshaping the streaming landscape by altering the way content creators generate income. The launch of Digitalage is anticipated to have a substantial impact on the economics of creators across the industry.

data.title: Dr. Scott Kamelle Scholarship for Future Doctors Introduced to Support Aspiring Medical Leaders Through Innovation and Academic Excellence
data.description: The scholarship cycle for the prestigious Dr. Scott Kamelle Scholarship for Future Doctors, established by Dr. Scott Kamelle, is now open for 2026. This scholarship presents undergraduate and potential medical students with a chance to further their academic pursuits. The scholarship provides a significant opportunity for students pursuing careers in the medical field. Applications for the scholarship are currently being accepted.

data.title: INTEGRAcare Inc named #1 Best Workplace in Healthcare in Canada and #7 Best Workplace in Canada
data.description: In Toronto on April 2, 2026, a renowned organization, Great Place to Work®, unveiled the top 50 Best Workplaces™ in Canada with staff ranging from 100 to 999 employees during an awards event. Among them, INTEGRAcare Inc. stood out as the #1 Best Workplace in Healthcare in the country. This recognition cements INTEGRAcare Inc.'s reputation for excellence in fostering a positive work environment.

data.title: Project Costing Gaps Leave IT Leaders Unable to Prove Value or Defend Spend, Finds Info-Tech Research Group
data.description: Increasing expenses and changing market dynamics are compelling IT executives to provide more accurate justifications for technology expenditures. Despite this, numerous companies continue to use inadequate models that fail to consider total cost of ownership, risk factors, and the lasting benefits of investments. Recent findings from Info-Tech reveal that organizations must adopt more comprehensive approaches to evaluating technology investments, considering all these factors to make informed decisions.

data.title: Cresco Labs Awarded Texas Medical Cannabis License
data.description: Cresco Labs Inc., a prominent player in the cannabis industry with a portfolio of leading brands and Sunnyside dispensaries, has been provisionally granted a Texas Compassionate Use Program License. This vertically integrated license permits Cresco Labs (CSE: CL) (OTCQX: CRLBF) (FSE: 6CQ) to engage in the cultivation, processing, and distribution of medical cannabis in Texas. The Company's achievement of this license will give Texas patients additional access to medical cannabis products.

data.title: Vericel Announces BARDA Award Valued at up to $197 Million for Procurement and Advanced Development of NexoBrid
data.description: Vericel Corporation, a leading provider of advanced therapies for sports medicine and severe burn care, has secured a lucrative ten-year contract from the U.S. Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority (BARDA), a division of the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (ASPR) under the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS). This contract, totaling up to $197 million, includes various components such as the procurement of NexoBrid®, implementation and upkeep of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) system, creation and validation of a manufacturing facility in the U.S., and the enhancement of NexoBrid with a next-generation formulation and new indication.

data.title: EXL announces a strategic services collaboration with Google Cloud
data.description: The partnership between EXL and Google Cloud highlights EXL's expertise as a leading provider of AI and cloud transformation solutions. This collaboration emphasizes EXL's position as a unique partner focused on delivering tangible outcomes.

data.title: Kura Sushi Likely To Report Wider Q2 Loss; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description: Kura Sushi USA, Inc. (NASDAQ: KRUS) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings on April 7. Analysts are projecting a quarterly loss of 20 cents per share, compared to a loss of 14 cents per share in the same period last year. The expected quarterly revenue for the Irvine, California-based company is $77.59 million, up from $64.89 million reported last year. Claudia Schaefer was recently elected to Kura Sushi's board of directors. The company's stock price dropped by 2.4% to $67.71. For more details and analyst ratings, interested readers can visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Ray 2.55 update introduces fault tolerance for large-scale AI models, reducing downtime risk for distributed inference systems.
data.description: An update to Anyscale's Ray Serve has enabled DP group fault tolerance for vLLM WideEP deployments, reducing downtime risk for distributed AI inference systems. This update is crucial for maintaining system stability and ensuring uninterrupted AI processing across a network. The new feature enhances the reliability of the deployment by incorporating fault tolerance measures for DP groups, significantly reducing the risk of downtime. This advancement is particularly significant for distributed AI systems, where even minor interruptions can have a considerable impact. Overall, the update aims to improve the efficiency and dependability of Anyscale's Ray Serve system, benefiting organizations relying on distributed AI for their operations.
